name: env-local
purpose: Local development secrets: GitHub PAT, SonarCloud token, Context7 API key, Firebase config for MCP server tokens.
notes: SECURITY FLAG: contains live GitHub PAT, SonarCloud token, Context7 API key. Gitignored. .env.local.example is the portable template counterpart.

name: env-local-encrypted
purpose: Encrypted binary backup of .env.local secrets for safe storage alongside repo.
notes: Binary encrypted file; gitignored. Encryption method not documented in repo. Treat as opaque machine artifact.

name: env-local-example
purpose: Template for .env.local with placeholder values, Firebase domain/project-id patterns, and inline setup instructions.
notes: Structure fully portable; Firebase/Sentry keys are SoNash-specific. JASON-OS could adopt a trimmed version with just the MCP token block.

name: env-production
purpose: Production Firebase SDK configuration baked into the Next.js production build (NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars).
notes: SECURITY FLAG: contains live Firebase API key and App ID for sonash-app project. These are NEXT_PUBLIC_ so technically client-safe, but should be rotated if repo ever goes public. Not portable — SoNash project-specific.

name: firebaserc
purpose: Firebase CLI project alias — maps 'default' to sonash-app project ID.
notes: Three-field file: projects.default, targets, etags. Entirely project-specific.

name: gitattributes
purpose: Line-ending normalization: auto-detect for all files, force LF for shell scripts and .husky/* hooks.
notes: Minimal 4-line file. Portable as-is. JASON-OS already has .husky/ so the eol=lf rule for .husky/* is directly applicable.

name: gitignore
purpose: Git ignore rules covering Next.js build artifacts, Firebase, env files, Claude Code session state, test results, GSD plugin files, and security audit artifacts.
notes: Contains JASON-OS-portable sections (Claude Code state patterns, husky hooks block, research artifact rules) mixed with SoNash product sections (Next.js, Firebase, dataconnect). The Claude Code state section (~30 lines) is highly portable for JASON-OS.

name: lsp-json
purpose: LSP configuration mapping TypeScript/JavaScript extensions to typescript-language-server.
notes: Minimal 12-line file. Portable for any TypeScript project. JASON-OS does not currently have LSP config.

name: markdownlint-json
purpose: Markdownlint rule configuration — permissive profile disabling many rules to accommodate CLAUDE.md, skill docs, and technical documentation formats.
notes: Turns off ~25 rules. Rationale is Claude Code skill/doc formats don't conform to standard markdown conventions. Directly portable to JASON-OS.

name: markdownlintignore
purpose: Markdownlint ignore patterns excluding docs/archive, .claude skill/state/hook/agent dirs, and node_modules.
notes: Pattern set is largely portable; docs/archive/ is SoNash-specific.

name: mcp-json
purpose: Claude Code MCP server config — memory server (auto-managed) and sonarcloud custom server pointing to local script.
sanitize_fields: sonarcloud server path — scripts/mcp/sonarcloud-server.js is SoNash-specific
notes: Active tracked file. Note annotation: auto-discovered MCPs (Firebase, GitHub, Context7) are NOT here. Only the non-auto-discoverable servers appear. memory server entry is fully portable. Contains env var reference ${SONAR_TOKEN} in .mcp.json.bak.

name: mcp-json-bak
purpose: Backup of prior .mcp.json — uses ${SONAR_TOKEN} env-var substitution pattern with sonarcloud server; predates current .mcp.json.
notes: Backup file. Pattern of env-var substitution (${SONAR_TOKEN}) is the portable model for secret injection.

name: mcp-json-example
purpose: Comprehensive MCP server example template with 9 server entries, inline setup docs, and empty-string secret placeholders.
notes: This is the richer .mcp template. Contains security best-practice documentation inline. Highly valuable for JASON-OS as onboarding template.

name: mcp-json-example-root
purpose: Root-level MCP example (older format, fewer inline docs than .mcp.json.example) — 9 servers including ccusage, firebase, sonarcloud, github, filesystem, puppeteer, playwright, context7, nextjs-devtools.
notes: Parallel to .mcp.json.example but older format; both exist. Consolidation opportunity: one canonical example. Portable after path substitution.

name: nvmrc
purpose: Node.js version pin: 22 (LTS). Used by fnm/nvm for consistent Node runtime.
notes: Single character file: '22'. Matches JASON-OS Node version. Already present in JASON-OS.

name: oxlintrc-json
purpose: Oxlint fast-linter configuration — 6 rules (no-unused-vars, misused-promises, etc.) with ignorePatterns for build/test dirs.
notes: Oxlint is a Rust-based fast linter used as lint:fast. Rules are generic. JASON-OS could adopt without the SoNash-specific ignore path.

name: prettierrc
purpose: Prettier formatting config — semi, double-quotes, 2-space indent, printWidth 100, LF line endings, special MD override (80 chars, proseWrap:always).
notes: Generic formatting config. Portable as-is. JASON-OS doesn't currently have .prettierrc.

name: prettierignore
purpose: Prettier ignore patterns — build dirs, Firebase, lock files, IDE, semgrep test files, .research/ analysis artifacts.
notes: .research/ exclusion and lock file exclusion are directly portable. Firebase-specific paths need removal for JASON-OS.

name: test-baseline-json
purpose: Registry of scripts without test coverage — tracks path, line count, and notes for 80+ scripts across hooks, skills, and scripts dirs. Created 2026-03-10.
notes: SoNash-specific technical debt registry. The structure/pattern is portable (version, description, entries array with path/lines/note) but content is entirely SoNash-specific. JASON-OS could adopt the schema pattern for its own test baseline.

name: eslint-config-mjs
purpose: ESLint flat config — TypeScript, React Hooks, security plugin, custom sonash/* plugin rules for file I/O safety, code quality, React/JSX, and test quality. 14 rule blocks with CC limit enforcement.
notes: Tightly coupled to eslint-plugin-sonash custom rules (sonash/*). Not portable without the plugin. The sonash/* rule taxonomy (no-raw-error-log, no-unguarded-file-read, etc.) maps to JASON-OS security guidelines and should be ported as a package.

name: firebase-service-account-json
purpose: Firebase Admin SDK service account credentials for sonash-app — used by Cloud Functions and admin scripts for privileged Firebase access.
notes: SECURITY FLAG CRITICAL: File contains live RSA private key and service account for sonash-app. Listed in .gitignore — should NOT be committed. Verify this file is gitignored on all branches. DO NOT port or include in any sync. Per .gitignore: 'firebase-service-account.json' and 'serviceAccountKey.json' are both gitignored.

name: firebase-json
purpose: Firebase project deployment config — hosting (static export to out/), functions (nodejs22, functions/ source), Firestore rules/indexes, Storage rules with security headers.
notes: Security-conscious headers: HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options, Permissions-Policy, Referrer-Policy. Pattern for security headers is portable as reference. Deployment config is SoNash-specific.

name: firestore-indexes-json
purpose: Firestore composite index definitions for journal, security_logs, admin_jobs, quick_links, prayers, meetings, users collections.
notes: 12 compound indexes + 4 field overrides. Entirely SoNash data model specific.

name: firestore-rules
purpose: Firestore security rules — user-owned profile/journal/daily_logs/inventoryEntries, public collections (meetings, sober_living, daily_quotes), admin-only writes via Cloud Functions, rate_limits locked to Admin SDK.
notes: Security model: all writes to sensitive collections blocked (create/update: if false) forcing Cloud Function path. Pattern (isSignedIn, isOwner, isAdmin helpers) is reusable but data model is SoNash-specific.

name: storage-rules
purpose: Firebase Storage rules — deny-by-default, user-owned files at /users/{userId}/ only.
notes: Minimal 25-line file. Pattern is generic Firebase Storage best practice. SoNash context required for deployment.

name: framer-motion-d-ts
purpose: TypeScript module augmentation for framer-motion v12 + React 19 compatibility — adds MotionProps interface with full prop set.
notes: Product type shim for version incompatibility. Only relevant to Next.js/React apps using framer-motion v12.

name: web-speech-api-d-ts
purpose: Web Speech API TypeScript declarations augmenting Window with SpeechRecognition and webkitSpeechRecognition constructors.
notes: Product-specific type declaration. Only relevant for SoNash voice input feature.

name: components-json
purpose: shadcn/ui configuration — new-york style, RSC, Tailwind CSS v4, CSS variables, lucide icons, component/utils aliases.
notes: SoNash product UI config. Not portable to non-Next.js/shadcn projects.

name: knip-json
purpose: Knip unused dependency/export detector — entry points in app/components/lib, Next.js entry pattern, ignore list for 24 packages with known false positives.
notes: SoNash-specific ignore list. Pattern is portable but paths and ignoreDependencies are product-specific.

name: next-env-d-ts
purpose: Next.js TypeScript environment type reference file (auto-generated by next build).
notes: Auto-generated; listed in .gitignore. Next.js-specific.

name: next-config-mjs
purpose: Next.js configuration — static export mode (for Firebase Hosting), unoptimized images, loads .env.local via dotenv.
notes: Minimal config. The dotenv.config() call to load .env.local manually is a notable pattern for Next.js + Firebase hosting combo.

name: postcss-config-mjs
purpose: PostCSS config with @tailwindcss/postcss plugin (Tailwind v4 integration).
notes: Minimal 7-line config. SoNash product (Tailwind v4).

name: tsconfig-json
purpose: TypeScript config — strict mode, Next.js plugin, path alias @/*=./*, ESNext module, bundler resolution, includes .next types.
notes: Next.js-tailored tsconfig. Not portable to non-Next.js projects.

name: tsconfig-test-json
purpose: Test-specific TypeScript config — extends tsconfig.json, CommonJS output, noEmit:false, outDir:dist-tests, includes specific test files for type-checked compilation.
notes: Explicitly enumerates included test file paths (SoNash-specific).

name: tsconfig-tsbuildinfo
purpose: TypeScript incremental build cache (auto-generated).
notes: Auto-generated. Listed in .gitignore (*.tsbuildinfo). Machine artifact.

name: geocoding-cache-json
purpose: Geocoding result cache — maps Nashville-area street addresses to lat/lng coordinates for meeting finder feature.
notes: Cached product data. Contains real Nashville addresses. Not portable. Should probably be in data/ rather than root. Appears to be a committed cache to avoid geocoding API calls.

name: package-json
purpose: NPM package manifest — 90+ npm scripts covering testing, linting, auditing, reviews, session lifecycle, metrics, planning, security; Next.js + Firebase + React 19 + shadcn/ui dependency graph.
notes: The npm scripts section (90+ entries) is the most portable artifact here — JASON-OS could adopt patterns for: test, lint, docs, audit, session lifecycle, review, security. Deps are Next.js/Firebase product-specific.

name: package-lock-json
purpose: NPM lockfile for deterministic dependency resolution.
notes: Auto-generated. Not portable.

name: husky-pre-commit
purpose: Pre-commit gate script — runs lint-staged, pattern compliance, doc index, debt validation, tests (skippable), cross-doc checks. Sources _shared.sh for SKIP_CHECKS infrastructure. Wave 3 timing + hook-runs.jsonl telemetry.
sanitize_fields: SoNash-specific check scripts (patterns, doc-index, debt, cross-doc)
notes: 10KB+ script. Significantly more complex than JASON-OS pre-commit (1.4KB). Key portable infrastructure: SKIP_CHECKS variable consolidation, SKIP_REASON validation, timing with date +%s%N, hook-runs.jsonl telemetry, add_exit_trap pattern, HOOK_OUTPUT_LOG for CI. JASON-OS pre-commit is much simpler.

name: husky-pre-push
purpose: Pre-push gate — escalation gate (unacked warnings), propagation check, CC check, trigger check, reviewer check; sources _shared.sh.
sanitize_fields: SoNash-specific check scripts (propagation, triggers, reviewer, CC)
notes: 33KB script — largest husky hook. Escalation gate (wave 0: blocks on unacked warnings) is highly portable. JASON-OS pre-push is simpler (5KB) but shares the block-push-to-main pattern.

name: husky-post-commit
purpose: Post-commit auto-resolver — runs scripts/resolve-hook-warnings.js in best-effort mode to clear stale warnings after a fix commit.
notes: 28-line script. The resolver is SoNash-specific but the pattern (post-commit warning resolver, best-effort exit 0) is portable. JASON-OS does not have a post-commit hook.

name: husky-shared-sh
purpose: Shared hook infrastructure — add_exit_trap (POSIX EXIT trap chaining), is_skipped (SKIP_CHECKS CSV lookup), require_skip_reason (validation with injection protection), _shared_init_fnm (fnm Node version loading).
sanitize_fields: _shared_init_fnm may contain SoNash-specific Node setup paths
notes: 10KB portable shell library. Most valuable husky artifact for JASON-OS. JASON-OS has simpler _shared.sh (2KB). Key additions: SKIP_CHECKS/SKIP_REASON infrastructure, multi-line SKIP_REASON injection protection, control-char filtering, fnm integration. Strong candidate for direct port.

name: husky-gitignore
purpose: Gitignore for husky internal state dir (.husky/_/).
notes: Single-character gitignore from husky v9 setup. Auto-generated by husky.

name: vscode-settings
purpose: VS Code workspace settings — git ignore limit warning, SonarLint connected mode (projectKey: jasonmichaelbell78-creator_sonash-v0), claudeCode.useTerminal:true.
notes: Contains SonarCloud project-specific binding. The claudeCode.useTerminal:true setting is portable. Note: .vscode/ is listed in .prettierignore but tracked in git.

name: vscode-mcp
purpose: VS Code MCP server config (not Claude Code) — context7, puppeteer, playwright via ensure-fnm.sh wrapper; context7 uses promptString for API key.
notes: Gitignored per .gitignore ('# MCP configuration (VS Code - not used by Claude Code)' with .vscode/mcp.json listed). VS Code-specific. Note ensure-fnm.sh wrapper pattern for VS Code MCP is portable.

name: gemini-config
purpose: Gemini Code Assist config — have_fun:false, ignore_patterns for generated/archive dirs, memory enabled, code review at MEDIUM severity, unlimited comments, skip draft PRs.
sanitize_fields: ignore_patterns paths (docs/technical-debt, docs/audits, docs/aggregation, docs/archive, scripts/consolidation-output are SoNash-specific)
notes: Core review settings (severity MEDIUM, unlimited comments, skip drafts, memory enabled) are fully portable. Created 2026-02-27. JASON-OS does not have .gemini/ configured.

name: gemini-styleguide
purpose: SoNash code review style guide for Gemini — stack versions, architecture rules (repository pattern, security model, state management), critical anti-patterns, security matrix for Cloud Functions, test standards.
notes: Version 1.2, updated 2026-03-30. SoNash-specific stack versions and architecture. The critical anti-patterns section (raw error.message, path traversal startsWith bypass, direct Firestore writes) maps directly to JASON-OS anti-patterns. Pattern for writing a project styleguide is portable even if content is not.

name: COMMAND_REFERENCE-md
purpose: Lightweight index of all Claude Code skills and commands (trimmed from 109KB to index format per AI-5.1). Links to source files.
notes: Version 6.1, updated 2026-04-15. ~100+ skill/command entries. The index-format approach (linking to SKILL.md sources vs. duplicating content) is a portable methodology for JASON-OS's COMMAND_REFERENCE. Content is SoNash-specific.

name: CROSS_PLATFORM_SETUP-md
purpose: Cross-platform Claude Code setup guide — explains settings sync between Windows CLI and Claude.ai Web. References removed sync script with DEPRECATED annotations.
notes: DEPRECATION NOTICE inline: sync script removed 2026-02-23. Document describes manual settings.global-template.json merge pattern. The cross-platform problem (Windows CLI vs Linux web) is highly relevant to JASON-OS. Document should be updated but the problem statement is portable.

name: HOOKS-md
purpose: Documentation of all Claude Code hooks in settings.json — event types, hook configurations, and behavioral descriptions.
notes: Version 2.0, updated 2026-02-23. Mentions 5 event types including UserPromptSubmit (not in SCHEMA_SPEC — SoNash-specific event?). The doc format and structure is portable; hook content is SoNash-specific.

name: REQUIRED_PLUGINS-md
purpose: List of required Claude Code plugins (21 claude-code-workflows + additional plugins) with setup instructions for merging global template.
sanitize_fields: SoNash-specific plugin list items that won't apply to JASON-OS
notes: References sync-claude-settings.js (REMOVED). 21 claude-code-workflows plugins + others. Plugin list is subjective — JASON-OS should define its own. The pattern (enumerate required plugins, explain how to merge into ~/.claude/settings.json) is portable.

name: STATE_SCHEMA-md
purpose: Hook and session state file schema documentation — .claude/hooks/ ephemeral state and .claude/state/ persistent state. STATUS: STALE (covers ~15 of 113 files as of 2026-04-05).
notes: Version 1.3, updated 2026-04-05. STALE annotation: documents 15/113 state files. 113 state files is a SoNash-specific data point. The two-tier architecture pattern (hooks/ ephemeral vs state/ persistent) and schema documentation methodology are portable for JASON-OS.

name: settings-json
purpose: Claude Code project settings — permissions (allow/deny lists), env vars, MCP server config, hook wiring for SessionStart/PreToolUse/PreCompact/PostToolUse events, ~15+ hooks registered.
notes: enableAllProjectMcpServers:true. Deny list includes git push --force, git push origin main, git reset --hard, rm -rf. The deny list is highly portable for JASON-OS. CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 enables agent teams feature.

name: settings-global-template-json
purpose: Global Claude Code settings template — model:opus, alwaysThinkingEnabled, GSD plugin hooks, statusline command, 21+ plugin enables. Intended to be merged into ~/.claude/settings.json.
notes: The global template pattern (ship a template for ~/.claude/settings.json with merge instructions) is directly portable. JASON-OS should adopt this pattern for distributing global settings recommendations.

name: tool-manifest-json
purpose: Preferred CLI tool manifest — 14 modern CLI tools (fzf, bat, fd, delta, zoxide, eza, rg, starship, yazi, lazygit, yq, gron, htmlq, difft) with prefer_over aliases and categories.
notes: Version 1. Fully portable. Informs Claude Code hooks about available modern CLI tools. JASON-OS should adopt this directly.

name: alerts-acknowledged-json
purpose: Alert system session state — records last alerts run result with grade A, score 92, 10 alerts, decision counts (fixed/deferred/ignored).
notes: SoNash session state. The JSON schema (lastRun, mode, grade, score, totalAlerts, decisions) is portable for JASON-OS alert system if adopted.

name: hook-warnings-json
purpose: Active hook warning registry — single S0 TDMS debt warning with occurrences tracking. Written by session-start hook, read by pre-push escalation gate.
notes: SoNash-specific content (TDMS debt warning). The schema (warnings array with hook/type/severity/message/action/timestamp/occurrences/occurrences_since_ack) is portable for JASON-OS hook warning system.

name: mcp-global-template-json
purpose: Global MCP server template — ccusage (bunx) and playwright servers. Intended for ~/.claude/mcp.json.
notes: Minimal 17-line template. ccusage entry is fully portable. playwright entry uses ensure-fnm.sh wrapper (SoNash hook). Pattern is portable.

name: override-log-jsonl
purpose: Audit trail of SKIP_CHECKS overrides — records timestamp, check name, reason, user, cwd, git_branch for each gate bypass.
notes: Contains real override history. Entries reference 'C:\\Users\\Owner\\workspace\\sonash' — earlier machine paths visible. The schema and concept (audit trail for gate bypasses) is highly portable for JASON-OS.

name: session-activity-jsonl
purpose: Session activity log — records session_start events with timestamp, user, outcome, source.
notes: Gitignored per .gitignore (.claude/session-activity*.jsonl). Tracked version may be stale. Session telemetry data.

name: settings-local-json
purpose: Machine-local Claude Code permissions — Go binary path permissions for Windows (c:/Program Files/Go, c:/Users/jason/...). Not committed.
notes: Gitignored. Contains absolute Windows paths for Go binary permissions. Machine-specific. The pattern (local overrides for machine-specific tool paths) is useful for JASON-OS documentation.

name: claude-config-dir
purpose: Project configuration files for Claude Code hooks — high-churn-watchlist.json, hook-audit-suppressions.json, propagation-intentional-divergence.json.
sanitize_fields: All three files are SoNash-specific but the pattern (config/ dir for hook tunables) is portable
notes: 3 files: high-churn-watchlist.json (files exempt from propagation checks), hook-audit-suppressions.json (suppression registry), propagation-intentional-divergence.json (approved divergences). Pattern: separate config dir for hook configuration overrides — portable for JASON-OS.

name: claude-state-dir
purpose: Persistent session-surviving state — 127 files (78 JSON, 25 JSONL, 6 MD, 2 archive, etc.) covering skill states, commit logs, audit histories, agent token usage, review data.
notes: 127 files. SoNash-specific content. STATE_SCHEMA.md covers ~15 files. The naming conventions (skill-name.state.json, skill-history.jsonl) are portable for JASON-OS.

name: claude-multi-ai-audit-dir
purpose: Multi-AI audit session state — single session-state.json file. Gitignored.
notes: Gitignored. Contains one session state file. Ephemeral runtime artifact.

name: claude-plans-dir
purpose: Planning documents — archive/ of past plans, named planning subdirs (automation-gap-closure, learning-system-overhaul), and MD files (hook-systems-audit-implementation, learning-effectiveness-analyzer, pr-ecosystem-audit-plan).
notes: SoNash planning artifacts. Pattern: co-locate plans under .claude/plans/ rather than .planning/. JASON-OS uses .planning/ at root. This is a divergence.

name: claude-test-protocols-dir
purpose: Test protocol JSON files for different admin/auth feature areas (~10 enumerated: admin-analytics, admin-content, admin-dashboard, auth-anonymous, etc.).
sanitize_fields: All protocol files are SoNash product-specific
notes: Pattern: structured test protocols in .claude/test-protocols/ mapped to feature areas. SoNash product content but pattern is portable.

name: claude-test-results-dir
purpose: Test suite results — smoke report MD, smoke JSONL, and screenshots directory. Gitignored.
notes: Gitignored per .gitignore. Generated test artifacts.

name: claude-tmp-dir
purpose: Temporary Claude Code runtime files — alerts JSON, audit results. Gitignored.
notes: Gitignored. Contains alerts-full-*.json, audit-result-final*.json. Ephemeral.

name: claude-worktrees-dir
purpose: Git worktree workspaces for research/planning tasks. Gitignored.
notes: Gitignored (.claude/worktrees/). Machine-local worktree directories for isolated research contexts.

name: claude-projects-dir
purpose: Claude Code project-scoped memory directory — mirrors ~/.claude/projects/ structure. Contains subdirectory C--Users-jason-Workspace-dev-projects-sonash-v0/ with memory files.
notes: Unusual: project-level .claude/projects/ mirrors user-home memory structure. May be a backup or sync artifact. Needs further investigation by D18 or D23.
